subject: Public Health
title: One Health: Humans Animals and Environment
description: A short lesson to help you apply One Health: Humans Animals and Environment.

hook.text: About 75% of new infectious diseases jump to humans from animals—zoonoses.

buildup.text: Deforestation pushes wildlife closer to humans, creating new pathways for viruses to spill over.

discovery.text: The One Health approach links human, animal, and environmental health as one interconnected system.

twist.text: Antibiotic resistance in farm animals spreads to humans through food and shared water sources.

climax.text: COVID-19, Ebola, and HIV all started as animal viruses—One Health aims to prevent the next one.

punchline.text: Healthy animals, healthy planet, healthy people.

quiz.question: What percentage of new infectious diseases come from animals?
